title: typhaceae
intro: The Typhaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as cattails or bulrushes. It consists of about 5-10 species of aquatic and wetland plants, characterized by their long, narrow leaves and distinctive brown or white seed heads. The Typhaceae family is often found in freshwater and brackish environments, where they provide habitat and food for various aquatic animals. These plants are widely distributed across the Northern Hemisphere, with some species also found in tropical and subtropical regions.
